sentenceFrame: Dog has the bone at home.
celebration: Magic-e reaches the letter o — three magic-e vowels now (a_e, i_e, o_e). Touch the red e to hop and it becomes hope, to not and it becomes note. And Dog — a friend since Week 12 — comes home to star, with a bone.

songs.title: Magic E Makes Bone
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(oh… oh… oh…)
Magic E! Magic E!

[Hook]
Hop… HOPE! Not… NOTE!
Magic E! Magic E!
Rob… ROBE! Cod… CODE!
The E is magic! (shhh — it's silent!)

[Verse 2]
Rod… RODE! Glob… GLOBE!
Magic E! Magic E!
The O says its NAME! (O! O!)
And the E says… (nothing!)

[Verse 3]
B-O-N-E — bone!
H-O-M-E — home!
N-O-S-E — nose!
(On the nose! On the nose!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No magic E!
(oh… oh… oh…)

[Outro]
The E is magic.
The E is silent. (shhh…)
BONE!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Transformation pairs on the beat (hop→HOPE, not→NOTE). The '(oh… oh… oh…)' stutters sing the LONG O sound /oʊ/ (phonetic respelling: sung 'oh', printed o_e). Spell-outs uppercase (B-O-N-E). Third week of the Magic E series (cake→bike→bone). 'The O says its NAME' is the lesson; the silent-e whisper gag closes it.

songs.title: The Bone at Home
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
Dog has the bone!
Dog has the bone!
Home, Dog, home! (home! home!)
Dog has the bone!

[Verse 2]
The bone is mine! (mine! mine!)
Home, Dog, home!
The bone is at home! (at home!)
Dog has the bone!

[Verse 3]
A rope! A rope! (tug! tug!)
The bone is on the nose?! (on the nose!)
Home, Dog, home! (home! home!)
Dog can nap at home!

[Outro]
The bone… the rope… (shhh…)
At home?! (a potato!)
(POTATO!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'Dog has the bone at home.' — Dog is Level-1 cast (Week 12), the class knows the name by heart. Verse 3's nose-balance is the visual gag; the 'Home, Dog, home!' command-frame carries the Level-1 pattern forward. Whisper the outro, potato hits loud.

spreads.artNote: A big clean bone alone in the spotlight, gleaming. Every dog's dream.
Dog — the friendly dog, cast since Week 12 — with the bone proudly in its jaws, eyes shining.
Dog sitting tall, chest out, guarding the bone between its paws. Comic pride.
Dog bounding toward a cosy little house on the hill, bone in mouth, ears flying.
Dog tucking the bone into a snug basket by the door. Home at last.
A coiled length of rope in the spotlight — a new temptation.
Dog with the rope in one side of its mouth and the bone in the other, torn between two treasures.

spreads.text: The bone is on the nose!
spreads.artNote: Dog balancing the bone flat on its nose, cross-eyed with concentration. Peak silliness.
The basket by the door — and where the bone was, a single potato sits, tucked in cosily. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. One of Dog's eyes cracks open.

sentences: Dog has the bone at home.
The bone is on the nose.
extras: THE MAGIC-E WAND, week 3: build hop, touch the red e — hop→hope, not→note, rob→robe, cod→code, rod→rode, pok→poke. The o changes its voice from /o/ to /oʊ/. Third vowel; same trick.
Pattern-card tracing continues: silent e greyed with a halo — trace, whisper nothing at the e.
Minimal-pair sort (short o vs long o): hop/hope, not/note, rob/robe, cod/code — hear the vowel flip.
Moveable alphabet with the special red e: b-o-n-e, h-o-m-e, n-o-s-e, r-o-p-e.

teacherFocus: MAGIC-E, WEEK 3 — the letter o. Wand pairs: hop→hope, not→note, rob→robe, cod→code, rod→rode, pok→poke. Do all six before new words. This is the third magic-e vowel — the mechanic is now familiar, so lean on the minimal pairs for the hearing work. Pattern-card tracing continues (silent e grey). Dog, a friend since Week 12, comes home to star — celebrate the returning cast member.
